title: Ensure that the --peer-client-cert-auth argument is set to true

input_row.title: Ensure that the --peer-client-cert-auth argument is set to true
input_row.description: etcd should be configured for peer authentication.
input_row.rationale: etcd is a highly-available key value store used by Kubernetes deployments for persistent storage of all of its REST API objects. These objects are sensitive in nature and should be accessible only by authenticated etcd peers in the etcd cluster.
input_row.audit: Run the following command on the etcd server node: ps -ef | grep etcd Verify that the --peer-client-cert-auth argument is set to true . Note: This recommendation is applicable only for etcd clusters. If you are using only one etcd server in your environment then this recommendation is not applicable.
input_row.remediation: Edit the etcd environment file (for example, /etc/etcd/etcd.conf ) on the etcd server node and set the ETCD_PEER_CLIENT_CERT_AUTH parameter to "true" : ETCD_PEER_CLIENT_CERT_AUTH="true" Edit the etcd startup file (for example, /etc/systemd/system/multi- user.target.wants/etcd.service ) and configure the startup parameter for --peer- client-cert-auth and set it to \"${ETCD_PEER_CLIENT_CERT_AUTH}\" : ExecStart=/bin/bash -c "GOMAXPROCS=$(nproc) /usr/bin/etcd --name=\"${ETCD_NAME}\" --data-dir=\"${ETCD_DATA_DIR}\" --listen-client-urls=\"${ETCD_LISTEN_CLIENT_URLS}\" --peer-client-cert-auth=\"${ETCD_PEER_CLIENT_CERT_AUTH}\"" Based on your system, reload the daemon and restart the etcd service. For example, systemctl daemon-reload systemctl restart etcd.service 121 | P a g e Impact: All peers attempting to communicate with the etcd server will require a valid client certificate for authentication.
input_row.default_value: Note: This recommendation is applicable only for etcd clusters. If you are using only one etcd server in your environment then this recommendation is not applicable. By default, --peer-client-cert-auth argument is set to false .

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. SSH into the etcd server node.
2. Run the command: `ps -ef | grep etcd`.
3. Verify that the `--peer-client-cert-auth` argument is set to `true` in the output of the command.
4. If the argument is not set to `true`, check the etcd environment file (e.g., `/etc/etcd/etcd.conf`) to ensure that the `ETCD_PEER_CLIENT_CERT_AUTH` parameter is set to `"true"`.
5. Additionally, check the etcd startup file (e.g., `/etc/systemd/system/multi-user.target.wants/etcd.service`) to confirm that the `--peer-client-cert-auth` parameter is configured correctly.
6. Ensure that you reload the daemon and restart the etcd service if any changes are made: 
   - Run `systemctl daemon-reload`
   - Run `systemctl restart etcd.service`
7. Confirm that all peers attempting to communicate with the etcd server require a valid client certificate for authentication.
